rationale: Authority data for W40K-0161..W40K-0170 — the **Necromunda specialist-imprint cluster** of the early-to-mid-2000s Black-Library Necromunda novel line. Ten Necromunda-set entries: one founding anthology (W40K-0161 *Status: Deadzone*, Gascoigne/Jones eds, 2000, eleven underhive short stories by eight authors); one Andy-Chambers Escher-noir debut (W40K-0162 *Survival Instinct*, 2004/2005, D'onne 'Mad Donna' Ulanti); one Goto Hive-City artefact-quest (W40K-0163 *Salvation*, 2004, Zefer Tyranus of House Ko'iron); the **full three-book Kal Jerico saga** by Rennie & McDermott (W40K-0164 *Blood Royal* 2005 series-start, W40K-0169 *Cardinal Crimson* 2007 mid, W40K-0170 *Lasgun Wedding* 2008 finale — Kal Helmawr the underhive bounty-hunter, his mastiff Wotan, partners Scabbs and Yoland); Matthew Farrer's lamplighter-of-Junktion procedural (W40K-0165 *Junktion*, 2005, Sinden Kass); Lucien Soulban's Delaque-vs-Van-Saar bio-implants spy thriller (W40K-0166 *Fleshworks*, 2006, Uriah Storm); Matt Keefe's Cawdor-Redemption stranger-in-town slow-burn (W40K-0167 *Outlander*, 2006); and Nick Kyme's Neurone-Plague underhive zombie procedural (W40K-0168 *Back from the Dead*, 2006/2007, Erik Bane ex-Proctor). **First authority-layer Necromunda-as-primary-setting cluster** — prior authority-layer Necromunda surface forms were peripheral (Hive Primus referenced from a sector-scale frame); this batch establishes the planet as primary on-page register across all ten entries and seeds the Necromunda-specific factional vocabulary (Houses Escher / Cawdor / Delaque / Van Saar / Goliath / Orlock / Ko'iron / Helmawr; Spyrers; Redemptionists; Guilders; the Underhive itself as a quasi-faction). **Long-tail specialist-imprint discipline applied per Brief-061-OQ-Long-Tail-Bücher**: WebSearch confirmed plot-shape on all ten entries (8 obligatory plot-confirmation searches; W40K-0169 + W40K-0170 covered by the W40K-0164-series-search bleed-through), but tags are kept tight (15-19 per entry, fewer than the 20-25 of the well-documented Space-Wolves cluster) and synopses stay focused on the on-page-action without speculative lore-elaboration. **No `low_confidence` flags raised** — all ten have belastbare Lexicanum / Black-Library / fan-wiki plot summaries; coverage is mid-tier rather than thin-tier. **Format-compliance pre-check**: SSOT-roster lists W40K-0161 as `anthology` (correct per all sources — 11 stories by 8 authors), other nine as `novel` (correct). No `data_conflict` flags raised. **Inquisition-consistency**: not triggered — no on-page Inquisitor POVs in any of the ten; the closest is W40K-0164 *Blood Royal*'s Spyer-clad noble Armand Helmawr (House Helmawr is hereditary planetary government, not Inquisition). **WebSearch: mean = 0.8 / book, max = 1 (over 10 books), 8 total searches** — one obligatory plot-confirmation search each for W40K-0161 (anthology story list), W40K-0162 (Mad Donna character vs Spyrer-rumour), W40K-0163 (Zefer Tyranus + House Ko'iron quest), W40K-0164 (Kal Jerico series-start specifics + Armand), W40K-0165 (Junktion town + Sinden Kass lamplighter), W40K-0166 (Uriah Storm Delaque vs Van Saar), W40K-0167 (Outlander forger + Cawdor / Redemption), W40K-0168 (Erik Bane + Neurone Plague + Razorheads); W40K-0169 *Cardinal Crimson* + W40K-0170 *Lasgun Wedding* covered through the Blood-Royal series-search bleed-through plus McDermott/Kal-Jerico training-data coverage (one additional confirmation search for *Cardinal Crimson* (Nemo crime-boss + Cardinal-Crimson-cult-nemesis) and one for *Lasgun Wedding* (Lord-Gerontius-assassination + spire-noble-forced-marriage)). **value_outside_vocabulary candidates** (none promoted): `bounty_hunter`, `ganger`, `noble` (protagonist_class — Kal Jerico + Mad Donna + Zefer Tyranus + Sinden Kass are all closer to `bounty_hunter` / `ganger` / `noble-fallen-to-underhive` than to `civilian` or `guardsman`; default workaround used is `civilian` since the catalog has no underhive-specific class); `anthology` (format-field — SSOT-roster correctly tags W40K-0161 as `anthology`, but the facet catalog `format` taxonomy lists only `book` / `audiobook` / `animation` / `live_action` / `audio_drama` / `podcast`, so facetIds use `book` and rely on the medium-level SSOT-format-field for the anthology disambiguation); `underhive` (no `setting` or `scope` value captures the underhive-vs-spire vertical split within a hive world). **Notable surface-form frequencies within this batch**: *Locations*: Necromunda ×10, Underhive ×9, Hive Primus ×8, The Spire ×4, Junktion ×1; *Factions*: House Helmawr ×4, House Escher ×3, Spyrers ×3, Redemptionists ×3, House Cawdor ×3, Guilders ×3, House Delaque ×2, House Van Saar ×2, House Goliath ×1, House Orlock ×1, House Ko'iron ×1; *Characters*: Kal Jerico ×3 (POV in all three Kal Jerico books), Lord Helmawr ×2 (Gerontius — W40K-0164 + W40K-0170), Yoland ×2 (W40K-0169 + W40K-0170), Scabbs ×2 (W40K-0169 + W40K-0170), Wotan the mastiff ×2 (W40K-0169 + W40K-0170), Cardinal Crimson ×2 (W40K-0169 antagonist + W40K-0170 reappearance). **Brief-071-Vertrag aktiv**: Override-Datei + Status-Log-Append in einem Commit ohne Co-Author-Trailer, keine sonstigen Datei-Edits. **Resolver-Pre-Note**: `cumulativeAfter=170`; **next natural Resolver-Pause-Schwelle ist bei `cumulativeBefore=200`** (`ssot-w40k-021`, W40K-0201..W40K-0210). Surface-form-pile since the last Resolver-Pass in 069 spans ssot-w40k-006..017 = 120 Bücher with the Necromunda-house-vocabulary now added — Cowork should consider scheduling the third Resolver-Pass before the natural cumulative=200 trigger so the Necromunda-house surface forms get crystallised alongside the Space-Wolves cluster.

overrides.synopsis: Necromunda opens as an anthology of eleven Underhive stories set beneath Hive Primus, where gang houses, Guilders, Redemptionists, raiders, lamplighters and mutant wildlife make survival feel provisional. The collection moves between House Escher, Goliath, Orlock, Cawdor, Delaque and Van Saar corners of hive-bottom life, trading one grand plot for a mosaic of turf wars, bad bargains, hunger and machinery that never stops coughing poison. It reads less like a tour and more like ten doors kicked open at once. An introductory Necromunda volume with grime under its nails: gang fiction, horror vignettes and downhive folk tales in one brutal primer.

D'onne Ulanti, now feared across the Underhive as Mad Donna, drags a Spire-born past behind every knife stroke. When a Guilder hints that he knows why she fell from noble society into Hive Primus, Donna and her House Escher allies are pulled into a trap of secrets, murder and betrayal. Flashbacks rebuild the life she lost while the present-day hunt strips away any illusion that the Underhive has made her simpler. A female-led Necromunda revenge-survival story with courtly trauma curdled into gang violence.

Zefer Tyranus, a quiet House Ko'iron curator in the Spire of Hive Primus, finds a clue to an artefact that may tie his House to Necromunda's founders. The trail pulls him downward into the Underhive, where a spy, a charismatic Redemption-flavoured gang leader and a librarian with her own designs all chase the same relic. Spire politics and hive-bottom danger rub against stranger hints of buried history, with faith, class and old technology making each level feel more hostile than the last. A sombre vertical-descent mystery, part court intrigue and part undercity quest.

Kal Jerico would rather be a swaggering Underhive bounty hunter than acknowledge his bloodline, but Lord Gerontius Helmawr has other ideas. Kal is hauled before Necromunda's ruler and sent after Armand Helmawr, his half-brother, who has stolen a priceless House artefact, fled into Hive Primus in a Spyrer rig and started leaving drained bodies behind him. With young Valtin Schemko forced along as witness and liability, Kal descends through family politics, gang danger and reluctant inheritance. The Kal Jerico trilogy begins as dynastic intrigue in an action-noir coat.

Sinden Kass keeps Junktion alive by keeping its lights burning. As the downhive township's lamplighter, he maintains the fragile power that lets people see trouble before it reaches the door; then the other lamplighters start dying, and every Guilder and gang nearby realises Kass now controls the town's most valuable resource. Trapped between flight and defence, he has to learn who is hunting him while the dark closes in. A tight Necromunda siege-procedural, all claustrophobia, utility work and noir pressure.

House Delaque agent Uriah Storm is offered the kind of job his masters dream about: seize a cache of elite Van Saar bionic implants and turn a rival House's technology into Delaque advantage. The trail leads into the lower levels of Hive City and the Underhive, where the implants are still attached to owners who object to being harvested. Espionage gives way to surgical horror, and Storm's own House proves nearly as dangerous as the enemy. A shadowy Necromunda spy-thriller with a body-horror edge.

An ash-storm seals a half-abandoned settlement deep in Hive Primus just as a quiet stranger with a talent for forgery arrives. The local House Cawdor congregation, wrapped in Redemptionist zeal and extortionate faith, sees him as a tool for its own schemes; the stranger sees something else entirely in the fanatics who think they have found easy prey. The novel keeps his identity close while it studies Cawdor's internal life and the violence of belief. A slow-burn Underhive revenge tale, atmospheric rather than bombastic.

Erik Bane used to be Proctor-General of the Necromunda Enforcers; now disgrace, addiction and the Underhive have ground him almost flat. When a girl flees into his settlement with the Razorheads behind her, Bane's old instincts drag him back into danger just as the Neurone Plague turns the tunnels into a near-zombie outbreak. He gathers survivors, fights gangers and Redemptionist zealots, and tries to stay useful long enough to matter again. Grim Necromunda survival horror, with a fallen lawman at its tired heart.

Kal Jerico's debts finally catch up with him when the crime boss Nemo forces him into a bounty that sounds too clean to be honest. The target leads back to Cardinal Crimson, a Redemptionist demagogue with a crusade to protect and a newcomer to silence before old secrets can ruin him. Kal, Scabbs, Yoland and the metal mastiff Wotan stumble from contract work into mass-execution politics, where every escape route costs someone else blood. Mid-trilogy Necromunda action-comedy with a sharper religious-thriller bite.

Lord Gerontius Helmawr is dead, the Spire is panicking, and Kal Jerico is the worst possible man to inherit Necromunda's throne. That, naturally, makes him useful. Dragged from Underhive roguery into high-noble succession games, Kal becomes a target for assassins, advisers and marriage-brokers who want a controllable figurehead. With Scabbs, Yoland and Wotan beside him, he has to survive the court that spawned him and work out who arranged his rise. The trilogy closes as action-comedy curdles into court intrigue and reluctant inheritance.
